channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Tanzania Achieves Significant Reduction in Drug Trafficking and Substance Abuse in 2025
item.description: Tanzania has seen a notable improvement in its fight against drugs, reporting a 55.97 percent decrease in cannabis seizures during 2025. This drop is linked to stronger enforcement, collaboration betw...

item.title: Pakistan reiterates firm support for Palestine, rules out Israel policy shift without independent Palestinian state
item.description: Pakistan’s Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar has reaffirmed that the country remains firmly committed to its longstanding position on Palestine and Gaza, stating clearly that there ...

item.title: Building Quality Nigerian Talent at Scale Requires Investment in Health, Education, and Workplaces
item.description: Na so e be, before person fit become real talent, e gats start as healthy, safe pikin wey dey get correct access to care and education. This article talk say the wahala wey Moniepoint dey face with 50...

item.title: WHO reports increase in suspected Ebola cases and deaths in DR Congo amid Bundibugyo outbreak
item.description: The World Health Organisation (WHO) has reported a concerning rise in suspected Ebola cases in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC), linked to the Bundibugyo strain. According to the latest update, ...

item.title: Chris Hansen responds to Robert Pattinson portrayal in upcoming 'Primetime' film
item.description: Chris Hansen, the former NBC Dateline journalist best known for hosting the true-crime series To Catch a Predator, has reacted to Robert Pattinson portraying him in a forthcoming A24 film titled Prime...

item.title: US appreciates Pakistan’s mediation efforts in Iran-US diplomatic engagement

item.title: CBSE reports scanning issues in new OSM evaluation system; 20 answer-sheet mix-ups and 13,000 manual evaluations flagged
item.description: The Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) has reported significant issues during the first-time implementation of its on-screen marking (OSM) system for board examinations. According to governme...

item.title: Trump Purchases UFC Stock While Promoting White House Birthday Event
item.description: President Donald Trump has purchased between $15,001 and $50,000 in stock of TKO Group Holdings, the parent company of the Ultimate Fighting Championship (UFC), coinciding with his promotion of a Whit...

item.title: Kenya Met issues 24-hour forecast warning of rainfall in 36 counties and strong winds in multiple regions

item.title: Atiku criticizes Tinubu’s three-year record, citing hardship, insecurity, and alleged misused funds

item.title: Understanding whether age-related frailty can be slowed or improved through lifestyle interventions
item.description: Age-related frailty is increasingly being understood not as an inevitable and permanent condition, but as a dynamic health state that can often be improved or slowed with the right interventions. Frai...

item.title: Nigeria’s 27 Years of Democracy: Progress, Challenges and Governance Outcomes
item.description: Nigeria returned to democratic rule on May 29, 1999, after long years of military rule, marking a major turning point in the country’s political history. Since then, the nation has sustained uninterru...

item.title: Free State soil study uses nematodes to monitor ecosystem health and climate change
item.description: Researchers at the University of the Free State (UFS) are investigating microscopic nematodes, also known as roundworms, found in soil across protected nature reserves in the Free State to better unde...

item.title: Uttar Pradesh Namami Gange Department Implements Fully Transparent Digital Transfers for 170 Officials
item.description: In a significant move to strengthen transparency and reduce corruption, the Uttar Pradesh government's Namami Gange and Rural Water Supply Department conducted a fully digital transfer exercise on Fri...

item.title: Concerns rise over abuse of Tanzanian domestic workers in Oman under Kafala system
item.description: Tanzanian domestic workers in Muscat, Oman, are reportedly facing serious abuse and exploitation under the country’s Kafala sponsorship system, according to recent reports. The system ties migrant wor...

item.title: Tanzania reports over 1,000 tonnes of narcotics seized in 2025 with mixed trends in drug trafficking enforcement

item.title: Rising EU–China trade tensions as Europe considers protective economic measures
item.description: The article examines growing economic and political tensions between the European Union and China, as Europe reassesses its heavy dependence on Chinese imports. European leaders and policymakers are i...
